carbon_policy_signal.item: EU emissions rose 2025; linear reduction trajectory at risk; Commission reviewing carbon backstop measures and MSR intake triggers for Q3 2026.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: EUA near-term volatility as policy response debated; potential MSR intake acceleration or backstop carbon price floor would shift fossil dispatch economics and power marginal cost.

freshness_caveat: Coal data stale as of 2025-12-26 (178 days old); Clean Dark stale as of 2025-12-31 (173 days old). Current Clean Spark and TTF/Power/Storage data current (0–4 days old as of 2026-06-22).
risk_flags: EU storage at 46.4% full, 14.5 pp below 5-yr seasonal — refill pace into autumn will determine winter pricing regime.
Asia outbidding EU for LNG cargoes (Politico); direct TTF upside if competition intensifies and winter storage fill fails.
EU emissions rose 2025 vs 2024; EUA policy response under review — near-term volatility as Commission debates carbon backstop measures.
Clean Spark data stale (as of 2025-12-31, 173 days old); latest snapshot (2026-06-22) shows 44.83 EUR/MWh at 94th-percentile, but underlying gas/power legs require current verification.

base.summary: TTF steady around 40 EUR/MWh; Clean Spark elevated on thermal merit; storage refill gradual.

downside.summary: Permian surge and US LNG export ramp deliver ample global supply; renewables forecast improves on mild weather.

upside.summary: Asia LNG bidding war intensifies; winter 2026–27 storage fill risk forces TTF and power curve higher.

themes: Gas supply tightening via Asia LNG competition
Clean Spark elevated, coal historically suppressed
Storage deficit threatens H2 refill trajectory
AI baseload demand narrative supports power curve
top_drivers: Clean Spark at 94th-percentile (44.83 EUR/MWh) — gas in-the-money vs coal (7th-percentile, 96 USD/t), supporting thermal dispatch.
EU storage 14.5 pp below seasonal average at 46.4% full — refill pace into autumn will anchor H2 price regime; LNG bidding war with Asia adds winter risk.
AI baseload demand narrative (data center lobby) supports power curve 2027–28; offsets some downside from renewables recovery (48.6% of load, 64th-percentile).
top_takeaway: Clean Spark at 94th-percentile (44.83 EUR/MWh) amid tight EU storage (14.5 pp below seasonal); LNG bidding war with Asia threatens winter fill and TTF upside.

watchlist.metric: EU LNG import bidding vs Asia
watchlist.why: Direct TTF driver; Asia outbid narrative tightens European supply and raises winter storage fill risk.
Q3 2026 Commission decision; weakening rules ease upstream capex/supply cost, with mid-stream gas pass-through uncertainty.
2025 emissions rose; linear reduction target at risk — backstop policy and MSR triggers may create near-term EUA volatility.
Baseload demand growth supports power/gas curve 2027–28; reduces downside risk if materialises, challenges green-only transition narrative.

watchlist.metric: Permian gas production and US LNG export capacity
watchlist.why: Surge to 60% above 2021 (2025); supports transatlantic arb and winter availability, creating structural downside risk to TTF on ample global supply.

watchlist_dated.name: European Council summit (19 June)
watchlist_dated.why: Trade/energy policy direction; watch for China, LNG, deregulation signals impacting gas/power outlook.
